name: AMBAR Restaurant, Clarendon

notes: All 39 frozen rows were adjudicated: 15 exact matches, 13 normalized matches, five service/formulation variants, five stale extras, and one descriptionless parser artifact.
The current asserted catalog contains 98 food and nonalcoholic formulations across 238 source presentations; 33 current formulations match frozen rows and 65 were omitted from the frozen catalog.
Alcohol-only rows and unique legacy dishes absent from the current May/June 2026 menu surfaces were excluded.

Sixty-four current formulations have direct restaurant-issued D/G/N/SF/E/S item labels, ten have fixed positive signals from restaurant-issued ingredient wording, and 24 remain unavailable from restaurant-issued evidence.
Twenty-three frozen rows had allergen mismatches: 20 underreported supported allergens, two mixed omitted and invented signals, and one overreported signal.

The manufacturer ingredient page for Heineken 0.0 supports an Ingredient Intelligence gluten signal from barley malt but is not promoted to restaurant-issued allergen evidence; no wheat signal is asserted from barley.

Reviewed Clarendon identity and address, current menu-surface selection, all current PDF legends, asterisk modification semantics, the mislabeled allergy wrapper, current/legacy joins, service-presentation consolidation, alcohol exclusions, generic-crawler artifacts, and final generated fields.
Nine focused catalog/reconciliation tests pass. All 22 captured artifact hashes match, and every item-level evidence id resolves.

The target-only repair is idempotent at target SHA-256 215e4bc1d39efd188d26fd1c5732c64e3171ea63a6903ce85379944ec4541d8c and preserved all 1,494 non-target restaurants at SHA-256 3b0c05cd0d8590a3627862663378306d9fa9fa4ee7c455b5b8e2d49badd0c915.

findings.summary: The frozen generic crawl mixed broad and stale surfaces, emitted one descriptionless child row, retained five stale formulations, and omitted 65 current Clarendon formulations.
findings.resolution: Rebuilt 98 current food and nonalcoholic formulations across 238 source presentations; removed one parser artifact and five stale rows.

findings.summary: Twenty-three frozen rows disagreed with current evidence: 20 underreported supported allergens, two mixed omitted and invented signals, and one overreported signal; Mushroom Pizza falsely mapped oyster mushroom to shellfish while omitting milk, wheat, and gluten.
findings.resolution: Replaced the frozen rows with current formulations, removed invented milk and shellfish signals, and added only allergens supported by direct current labels or current item wording.

findings.summary: The current menus use D=dairy, G=gluten, N=nuts, SF=shellfish, E=eggs, and S=sesame; an asterisk means the allergen can be modified, not that it is absent. Missing codes do not establish freedom from allergens outside the six-code legend.
findings.resolution: Kept starred allergens in default formulations, added wheat only where the formulation identifies wheat food, and never inverted absent codes into contains, free-from, or cross-contact claims.

findings.summary: The official page labeled Clarendon Allergy Brunch links a PDF titled and populated as Allergy Capitol and Clarendon - Lunch and Dinner.
findings.resolution: Did not use the linked PDF as brunch evidence; used it only as a supplemental semantics check for still-current matching lunch/dinner formulations and recorded the configured-source warning.

findings.summary: Heineken's manufacturer page identifies barley malt, but manufacturer evidence cannot be represented as restaurant-issued allergen evidence, and the generic Ingredient Intelligence alias incorrectly inferred wheat from barley.
findings.resolution: Kept the restaurant's product identity separate from the manufacturer ingredient disclosure; represented gluten only as Ingredient Intelligence, left restaurant allergen status unavailable, and suppressed the false wheat inference.

findings.summary: The frozen record displayed only Arlington, VA instead of the current Clarendon street address.
findings.resolution: Set the display address to 2901 Wilson Blvd, Arlington, VA 22201 and the guide to the current Clarendon menu index.

repairs.summary: Replaced the polluted 39-row crawl with 98 current Clarendon food and nonalcoholic formulations across 238 source presentations; corrected 23 frozen allergen results, removed one parser artifact and five stale rows, restored 65 omitted formulations, and preserved manufacturer evidence as Ingredient Intelligence.

notes: Current address: 2901 Wilson Blvd, Arlington, VA 22201.
Current a-la-carte, lunch, and dinner PDFs were created June 23, 2026; brunch, desserts, drinks, and happy-hour PDFs were created May 11, 2026.
The older vegan/vegetarian guide remains in the sitemap but is absent from the current menu index; unique legacy dishes were excluded.
No safe dedicated audit worktree exists because the checkout remains dirty, so no checkpoint commit was created.
